description:
Data Description
The dataset used for this project contains information on various aspects of employee demographics, job roles, satisfaction levels, and other work-related attributes. Below is a detailed description of the columns in the dataset:

- Employee No.: A unique identifier for each employee.
- Gender: The gender of the employee (e.g., Male, Female).
- Education: The education level of the employee (e.g., Bachelor’s, Master’s).
- Education Field: The field of study in which the employee obtained their education (e.g., Engineering, Marketing).
- Job Role: The job title or role of the employee (e.g., Sales Executive, Research Scientist).
- Department: The department in which the employee works (e.g., Sales, Research & Development).
- Age group: The age group of the employee (e.g., 20-30, 30-40).
- Work Location: The location or branch where the employee is working.
- Attrition: A binary variable indicating whether the employee has left the company (Yes/No).
- Attrition Date: The date on which the employee left the company, if applicable.
- Attrition Label: A numerical label representing attrition status (e.g., 1 for Yes, 0 for No).
- Dynamic Date: A date that represents a dynamic point in time relevant to the employee's status (e.g., last -evaluation date).
- Age: The age of the employee in years.
- Avg. Satisfaction Score: The average satisfaction score of the employee over a period of time.
- Employee Count: The count of employees in the organization at the time of data recording.
- Environment Satisfaction: A score representing the employee’s satisfaction with the work environment.
- Attrition Count: The number of times an employee has left or rejoined the company, if applicable.
- Job Involvement: A score representing the level of involvement or engagement the employee has in their job.
- Job Satisfaction: A score representing the employee’s satisfaction with their job.
- Monthly Income: The monthly salary of the employee.
- Percent Salary Hike: The percentage increase in the employee’s salary over the last appraisal period.
- Performance Rating: The performance rating of the employee during the last evaluation.
- Placeholder: A placeholder column, possibly for future data or a redundant column.
- Relationship Satisfaction: A score representing the employee’s satisfaction with interpersonal relationships at work.
- Work Life Balance: A score representing the employee’s satisfaction with their work-life balance.
- last_evaluation: The score from the employee's last performance evaluation.
- number_project: The number of projects the employee has worked on during the last period.
- average_monthly_hours: The average number of hours the employee worked per month.
- Work_accident: A binary variable indicating whether the employee has experienced a work-related accident (Yes/No).
- promotion_last_5years: A binary variable indicating whether the employee has been promoted in the last 5 years (Yes/No).

This dataset provides a comprehensive view of the employees, allowing for in-depth analysis and the development of a robust predictive model for attrition.

results.reason: direct proxy for health condition or disability
